      have h₄ : ∃ (f : U → ℕ), one_to_one f := by
        -- Use the surjection from ℕ to U to construct an injection from U to ℕ
        have h₅ : ∃ (f : ℕ → U), onto f := by
          -- This is a property of countable types
          exact h₃
        -- Use the surjection to build an injection
        obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h₅
        -- Use the axiom of choice to get a right inverse of the surjection
        have h₆ : ∃ (g : U → ℕ), f ∘ g = id := by
          classical
          exact ⟨fun x => Nat.find (hf x), fun x => by

```lean
theorem Theorem_8_1_5_3_type {U : Type} :
    ctble U ↔ ∃ (f : U → Nat), one_to_one f := by
  constructor
  · -- (⇒) If U is countable, then there exists a one-to-one function from U to ℕ.
    intro h
    have h₁ : Nonempty (U ↪ ℕ) := by
      -- Use the fact that U is countable to get an injection from U to ℕ.
      have h₂ : ctble U := h
      rw [ctble_def] at h₂
      exact h₂
    -- Extract the injection from U to ℕ.
    obtain ⟨f⟩ := h₁
    refine' ⟨f, _⟩
    -- The injection is one-to-one by definition.
    exact f.injective
  · -- (⇐) If there exists a one-to-one function from U to ℕ, then U is countable.
    intro h
    obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
    -- Use the fact that there is an injection from U to ℕ to show that U is countable.
    have h₁ : Nonempty (U ↪ ℕ) := by
      -- Construct an injection from U to ℕ using the given one-to-one function.
      refine' ⟨{ toFun := f, injective := hf }⟩
    -- Since there is an injection from U to ℕ, U is countable.
    rw [ctble_def]
    exact h₁

theorem Theorem_8_1_5_3_type {U : Type} :
    ct

by
  have h1 : ctble U ↔ ∃ (f : U → Nat), one_to_one f := by
    apply Iff.intro
    · -- Prove the forward direction: ctble U implies ∃ f : U → Nat, one_to_one f
      intro h
      -- Use the fact that if U is countable, there exists a bijection with Nat
      have h2 : ctble U := h
      -- Extract the function from the countable set to Nat
      obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h2
      -- Use this function as the desired injective function
      refine' ⟨f, _⟩
      -- Verify that the function is injective
      have h3 : one_to_one f := by
        intro a b hab
        -- Since f is injective, f a = f b implies a = b
        exact hf.1 hab
      exact h3
    · -- Prove the reverse direction: ∃ f : U → Nat, one_to_one f implies ctble U
      intro h
      -- Use the existence of an injective function to Nat to show U is countable
      obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
      -- Use the fact that if there is an injective function from U to Nat, U is countable
      have h2 : ctble U := by
        -- Use the countable typeclass to show U is countable
        exact ⟨f, ⟨hf, by
          -- Since Nat is countable, there exists a surjective function from Nat to U
          have h3 : ctble ℕ := by
            exact ⟨(fun n : ℕ => n), ⟨fun _ _ h => by simpa using h, by
              intro n
              exact ⟨n, rfl⟩⟩⟩
          -- Use the fact that if there is an injective function from U to Nat and a surjective function from Nat to U, then U is countable
          exact ⟨fun n => 0, by simp⟩⟩⟩
      exact h2
  -- Use the established equivalence to prove the theorem
  exact h1

  constructor
  · intro h
    have h₁ : ∃ (f : U → ℕ), Function.Injective f := by
      have h₂ : Nonempty (U ≃ (Set.Iio (0 : ℕ))) := by
        exact Set.countable_iff_subset_of_paramap (Classical.decEq U) |>.mp h (Set.univ : Set ℕ)
      dsimp at h₂
      cases' h₂ with h₂
      exact ⟨h₂.mpr, by simpa [Function.Injective] using h₂.injective⟩
    cases' h₁ with f hf
    refine' ⟨f, _⟩
    simpa [Function.Injective] using hf
  · intro h
    have h₁ : ∃ (f : U → ℕ), Function.Injective f := by
      cases' h with f hf
      refine' ⟨f, _⟩
      simpa [Function.Injective] using hf
    have h₂ : Set.Countable (Set.univ : Set U) := by
      obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h₁
      have h₃ : Set.Countable (Set.range f) := Set.countable_range f
      have h₄ : Set.Countable (Set.univ : Set U) := by
        have h₅ : Set.Countable (Set.univ : Set U) :=
          Set.Countable.mono (fun x _ => by
            have : x ∈ Set.univ := Set.mem_univ x
            have : f x ∈ Set.range f := Set.mem_range_self x
            simp_all [Set.Countable]
          ) h₃
        exact h₅
      exact h₄
    exact Set.countable_univ.mp h₂

by
  apply Iff.intro
  · -- Prove the forward direction: if U is countable, then there exists an injective function from U to ℕ
    intro h
    have h₁ : ∃ (f : U → ℕ), Function.Injective f := by
      -- Use the fact that U is countable to get an equivalence with ℕ
      rcases h with ⟨f⟩
      -- Use the injective part of the equivalence
      refine' ⟨fun x => Classical.choose (f x), _⟩
      intro x y hxy
      have h₂ := Classical.choose_spec (f x)
      have h₃ := Classical.choose_spec (f y)
      have h₄ : (Classical.choose (f x) : ℕ) = (Classical.choose (f y) : ℕ) := by simpa using hxy
      have h₅ : (Classical.choose (f x) : ℕ) ∈ f x := by
        exact Classical.choose_spec (f x) |>.1
      have h₆ : (Classical.choose (f y) : ℕ) ∈ f y := by
        exact Classical.choose_spec (f y) |>.1
      have h₇ : x = y := by
        have h₈ : (Classical.choose (f x) : ℕ) ∈ f x := h₅
        have h₉ : (Classical.choose (f y) : ℕ) ∈ f y := h₆
        have h₁₀ : (Classical.choose (f x) : ℕ) = (Classical.choose (f y) : ℕ) := h₄
        have h₁₁ : (Classical.choose (f y) : ℕ) ∈ f x := by
          rw [h₁₀] at h₈
          exact h₈
        have h₁₂ : f x = f y := by
          apply Set.ext
          intro z
          have h₁₃ : z ∈ f x ↔ z ∈ f y := by
            constructor <;> intro hz <;>
              (try simp_all) <;>
              (try aesop) <;>
              (try omega)
          exact h₁₃
        -- Use the fact that the sets are equal to deduce x = y
        have h₁₃ : x = y := by
          -- Since f x = f y and f is injective, x = y
          simp_all [Set.ext_iff]
          <;> aesop
        exact h₁₃
      exact h₇
    -- Obtain the injective function
    rcases h₁ with ⟨f, hf⟩
    -- Provide the injective function
    refine' ⟨f, _⟩
    exact hf
  · -- Prove the backward direction: if there exists an injective function from U to ℕ, then U is countable
    intro h
    rcases h with ⟨f, hf⟩
    -- Use the injective function to show that U is countable
    have h₁ : ctble U := by
      -- Use the fact that there is an injective function from U to ℕ
      -- to show that U is countable
      refine' ⟨fun x => ⟨f x, _⟩⟩
      intro x
      exact ⟨x, by simp_all⟩
    exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : ctble U → ∃ (f : U → Nat), one_to_one f := by
    intro h
    obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
    refine' ⟨fun x => f.val x, _⟩
    intro x y hxy
    have h' := hf
    simp [Function.Embedding.coe_inj] at h'
    have := hf.injective
    simp_all [h']
    <;> aesop
  
  have h₂ : (∃ (f : U → Nat), one_to_one f) → ctble U := by
    intro h
    obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
    have h₃ : ctble U := by
      -- Use the fact that there is an injection from U to ℕ to conclude that U is countable
      have h₄ : U ∼ Set.range f := by
        -- Show that U is equivalent to the range of f
        have h₅ : ∀ (x : U), ∃ (y : ℕ), f x = y := by
          intro x
          refine' ⟨f x, _⟩
          <;> simp
        -- Use the injection f to construct an equivalence
        refine' ⟨fun x => ⟨f x, by simp [Set.mem_range]⟩, _⟩
        -- Verify the equivalence by checking injectivity and surjectivity
        <;>
          (try simp_all [Function.Embedding.coe_inj]) <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try
            {
              refine' ⟨fun x => Classical.choose (h₅ x), _⟩
              intro x
              simp_all [Classical.choose_spec]
              <;> aesop
            }) <;>
          (try
            {
              intros
              simp_all [hf]
              <;> aesop
            })
      -- Since the range of f is countable, U is countable
      refine' (h₄.symm.trans (countable_range _))
    exact h₃
  
  exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩

      -- Use the lemma that provides an injection from U to ℕ if U is countable.
      exact ⟨fun x => f x, fun x y hxy => by simpa using hf (by simpa using hxy)⟩
    exact h₁
  · -- Backward direction: If there exists an injection from U to ℕ, then U is countable.
    intro h
    have h₁ : ctble U := by
      obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
      -- Use the lemma that provides a countability proof if there is an injection from U to ℕ.
      -- In Lean 4, `Function.Embedding` is used for injections, so we need to convert `hf` to this form.
      have h₂ : ∃ (f : U → ℕ), Function.Injective f := by
        refine' ⟨f, _⟩
        intro x y hxy
        exact hf (by simpa using hxy)
      -- Use the lemma to prove countability.
      exact ctble_of_injective f (by
        intro x y hxy
        exact (show Function.Injective f from by
          intro x y hxy
          exact hf (by simpa using hxy)) hxy)
    exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : (∃ (f : U → ℕ), OneToOnes f) → Ctbl U := by
    intro hf
    rcases hf with ⟨f, hf⟩
    have h₂ : Ctbl U := by
      -- Use the fact that a countable type can be embedded into ℕ
      have : Encodable U := by
        -- Since there is an injective function from U to ℕ, U is encodable
        have h₃ : ∀ (a b : U), f a = f b → a = b := by
          intro a b h
          apply hf
          exact h
        refine' encodable_of_injective f h₃
      -- Use the encodable instance to show that U is countable
      exact encodable_iff_countable.mpr ‹_›
    exact h₂
  
  have h₂ : Ctbl U → (∃ (f : U → ℕ), OneToOnes f) := by
    intro h
    -- Use the fact that a countable type can be embedded into ℕ
    have : Encodable U := by
      -- Since U is countable, it is encodable

    -- Use the encodable instance to construct an injective function from U to ℕ
    have h₃ : ∃ (f : U → ℕ), OneToOnes f := by
      use fun x => x.enc
      intro x y h
      simp_all [Encodable.injEq]
    exact h₃
  
  -- Combine the two implications to get the final result
  exact ⟨h₂, h₁⟩

  have h₁ : ctble U ↔ ∃ (f : U → ℕ), Injective f := by

      exact Iff.intro (fun h => by exact h.exists_embedding) (fun h => by
        exact h.choose.prop)
    have h₃ : Nonempty (U ↪ ℕ) ↔ ∃ (f : U → ℕ), Injective f := by
      constructor <;> intro h
      · obtain ⟨e⟩ := h
        refine' ⟨e, _⟩
        exact e.injective

        have h₄ : U ↪ ℕ := {

  rw [h₁]
  <;> simp [Function.Injective]
  <;>
  aesop
